rationale: EV-001 shows reg.exe (running at High integrity as corp\Administrator) explicitly adding the UserInitMprLogonScript registry value under HKCU\Environment, a well-known persistence mechanism that executes the specified payload at every user logon (A0 confirms the value was successfully written). The payload set is C:\Windows\System32\calc.exe, which is an atypical/placeholder binary commonly used in proof-of-concept or post-exploitation testing — a legitimate admin would have no reason to configure calc.exe as a logon script. The parent process is a bare cmd.exe with no command-line arguments, consistent with an interactive shell obtained during an intrusion. No benign administrative workflow explains this combination; the absence of any software deployment or configuration management context further reduces the likelihood of a legitimate explanation.
